findings.message: TASK-01’s stale-thread query keys off `threads.updated_at`, but the current `syncInbox` unchanged-thread path re-upserts the thread on every sync and refreshes `updated_at` even when the thread is still stuck (`apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/sync.server.ts:525-536`, `apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/sync.server.ts:291-314`). That means genuinely stuck `pending` threads can stay perpetually “fresh” and never enter the recovery set.

TASK-03’s core mechanism is not viable as written. The plan assumes a Cloudflare cron can hit an internal API route through the Worker `fetch` handler, but Cron Triggers invoke a `scheduled()` handler rather than performing an authenticated HTTP request. Since the plan explicitly rejects adding a scheduled handler, the automation path it chooses does not match the platform execution model.

TASK-03 is internally inconsistent about the route target. The deliverable and acceptance use `/api/internal/inbox-recovery`, while the implementation notes say the cron will hit `/__internal/inbox-recovery` at line 231. That leaves the actual route contract ambiguous before implementation starts.

review:
I’ve got the main repo-side mismatches. I’m verifying one platform assumption against Cloudflare’s official docs because TASK-03 depends on cron behavior, and that affects whether the route-based automation path is even feasible.
- CRITICAL: docs/plans/reception-inbox-inprogress-recovery/plan.md:131 TASK-01’s stale-thread query keys off `threads.updated_at`, but the current `syncInbox` unchanged-thread path re-upserts the thread on every sync and refreshes `updated_at` even when the thread is still stuck (`apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/sync.server.ts:525-536`, `apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/sync.server.ts:291-314`). That means genuinely stuck `pending` threads can stay perpetually “fresh” and never enter the recovery set.

- CRITICAL: docs/plans/reception-inbox-inprogress-recovery/plan.md:83 TASK-03’s core mechanism is not viable as written. The plan assumes a Cloudflare cron can hit an internal API route through the Worker `fetch` handler, but Cron Triggers invoke a `scheduled()` handler rather than performing an authenticated HTTP request. Since the plan explicitly rejects adding a scheduled handler, the automation path it chooses does not match the platform execution model.

- WARNING: docs/plans/reception-inbox-inprogress-recovery/plan.md:220 TASK-03 is internally inconsistent about the route target. The deliverable and acceptance use `/api/internal/inbox-recovery`, while the implementation notes say the cron will hit `/__internal/inbox-recovery` at line 231. That leaves the actual route contract ambiguous before implementation starts.

VERDICT: NEEDS_REVISION
SCORE: 6/10
